name: Embalse de Sierra Boyera y Belmez — circular desde Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, agricultural plains, and the presence of reservoirs. The region offers a mix of open countryside and routes connecting small towns. Elevations are generally moderate, making it suitable for various cycling abilities. Best touring cycling routes around Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de Sierra Boyera y Belmez — circular desde Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route features reservoir views and historical landmarks. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Granjuela from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo, a difficult 30.7 miles (49.4 km) path. It traverses varied terrain, including open landscapes and rural roads. Local touring cyclists also love the Peñarroja-Pueblonuevo – Ermita de San Isidro Labrador loop from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo, a 23.6 miles (37.9 km) trail leading through rolling hills and agricultural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 49 minutes. Touring cycling around Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo is defined by rural roads, reservoir views, and historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the region's landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy touring cycling route. The region generally features moderate elevations, making many routes accessible. For a gentle introduction, look for routes with lower elevation gains and shorter distances.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo traverse a varied landscape of rolling hills, agricultural plains, and rural roads. You'll also find routes that feature reservoir views, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363089148/embalse-de-sierra-boyera-y-belmez-circular-desde-penarroya-pueblonuevo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Sierra Boyera y Belmez — circular desde Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult routes available. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026431455/la-granjuela-from-penarroya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Granjuela from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a significant distance and varied terrain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Several routes pass by interesting landmarks. You might encounter historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5360266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5341723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of El Terrible Dog&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of Mining&lt;/a&gt; also offers a unique cycling experience through the region's industrial heritage.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroja-Pueblonuevo – Ermita de San Isidro Labrador loop from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40006440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez – Belmez Castle loop from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). Temperatures are milder and more comfortable for longer rides, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

Q: How long do the touring cycling routes typically take to complete?
A: The duration varies significantly by route and your cycling pace. For example, a moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363089148/embalse-de-sierra-boyera-y-belmez-circular-desde-penarroya-pueblonuevo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Sierra Boyera y Belmez — circular desde Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo&lt;/a&gt; takes around 2 hours, while longer, more difficult routes can take 4 hours or more.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often appreciate the diverse rural roads, scenic reservoir views, and the opportunity to explore historical sites.

Q: Are there any routes that offer particularly good viewpoints?
A: Many routes provide excellent views of the surrounding countryside, especially those that traverse rolling hills or pass by reservoirs. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Fuente Obejuna – Fuente Obejuna loop from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers scenic vistas as it winds through the landscape.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and villages where routes begin, including Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo itself. Look for public parking areas or designated spaces near the starting points of your chosen route.

Q: Are there opportunities to stop for refreshments along the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes are not always directly on the trails, many routes connect or pass through small towns and villages where you can find local establishments for refreshments. Planning your stops in advance, especially on longer routes, is advisable.
tips: The reservoir fed by the Guadiato River, built in 1974. At its bottom is an Iberian settlement from the 6th century BC. discovered in 2017 due to drought in the reservoir.

If you go through Belmez, you will surely see its castle, because it is visible from almost anywhere since it is located on a large limestone rock. The 13th century castle stands majestically on the hill, like a lookout for the town of Belmez. It can be accessed by stairs to enjoy the panoramic views of the mountains and even the Sierra Boyera Reservoir.
